title: tephrocactus articulatus var. polyacanthus
intro: Tephrocactus articulatus var. polyacanthus, also known as the Tree Cactus or Devil's Head, is a type of cactus plant characterized by its unique, branching stems covered in sharp, yellow to brown spines. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Mexican and Latin American folk remedies, for its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Tephrocactus articulatus var. polyacanthus are said to include wound healing, pain relief, and the treatment of digestive issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Herbal preparations of the plant may include infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its potential medicinal benefits.
